title: Hardcoded or Static IV/Nonce
description: Using a static or hardcoded initialization vector (IV) or nonce in symmetric encryption defeats the security of the cipher, allowing plaintext recovery through ciphertext analysis (OWASP A02:2021 / CWE-329).

finding.message: Hardcoded IV or nonce '{{token}}' in {{file}} at line {{line}}.

references: Generate a fresh random IV/nonce for every encryption operation using a CSPRNG.
For AES-GCM, use a 96-bit (12-byte) random nonce; for AES-CBC, use a 128-bit random IV.
Prepend the IV/nonce to the ciphertext so the decrypting party can extract it.
Never reuse a nonce with the same key in GCM/CTR modes — this is catastrophic.
